differential_diagnoses: Ectopic varices (jejunostomy-related or biliary-enteric anastomosis site)
Chronic hepatic venous outflow obstruction (Budd-Chiari syndrome)
next_steps: Hepatic arterial and venous phase CT angiography or MR angiography to evaluate for pseudoaneurysms and portal/venous anatomy
Selective digital subtraction angiography of the celiac and mesenteric arteries to rule out hemobilia and vascular malformations
Interventional radiology consultation for evaluation of portal hypertension and biliary-enteric vasculature
Repeat enteroscopy focusing on the hepaticojejunostomy site and surrounding mucosa
Measurement of serum CA 19-9 and CEA to evaluate for underlying malignancy
Surgical consultation for potential management of biliary-enteric anastomotic complications

differential_diagnoses: Ectopic varices at the hepaticojejunostomy site
Chronic hepatic venous outflow obstruction (Budd-Chiari syndrome)
next_steps: Selective hepatic artery and mesenteric digital subtraction angiography (DSA) to identify a vascular-biliary fistula
Comprehensive hypercoagulability and thrombophilia profile (including JAK2 mutation, Factor V Leiden, and antiphospholipid antibodies)
Endoscopic ultrasound (EUS) or balloon-assisted enteroscopy focusing on the hepaticojejunostomy anastomosis
Serum CA 19-9, CEA, and possibly alpha-fetoprotein levels to evaluate for occult malignancy
Interventional radiology consultation for management of portal venous stenosis and portal hypertension
Multidisciplinary hepatobiliary surgery consultation for potential definitive repair of the hepaticojejunostomy
